Tllfar 28-6-84 3-5-92 17-2-96 1C * >3-99 \Â»*r ^ New Delhi, the 26th April, 1999 G.S.R. 283(E).â€”In exercise of the powers conferred by clause (g) of sub-section (2) of section 60, road with section 198 and clause (a) of sub-section (2) of section 200, of the Railways Act, 1989 (24 of 1989), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules farther to amend the Indian Railways (Open Lines) General Rules, 1976, namely:â€” 1. (I) These rules may be called the Indian Railways (Open Lines) General (Third Amendment) Rules, 1999. (2) They shall come into force on t^e date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 1. In the Indian Railways (Open Lines) General Rules, 1976, in Chapter XV, rule 8 shall be renumbered as sub-rule (I) thereof and after sub-rule (1) as so numbered, the following sub-rules shall be inserted, namely :â€” '"(2) Mo work involving removal of any mil from the track shall be undertaken without traffic block, except as provided in sub-rule (3). (3) In emergent cases, the Engineering official not below the rank of Permanent Way Inspector Grade-Ill undertaking .such operations shall first bring the train to stop and advise the Driver of the train about the need to stop the train through a written Memorandum. The Engineering official shall simultaneously arrange to send a message to the Station Master for the need to block the track and obtain written confirmation of the same. In such emergent cases, work shall be commenced only after advising the Driver and bringing the train to a stop." Note :â€” The principal rules were published vide not! fication number GSR 445(E)* dated the 21 st July, 1981 and subsequently (1) GSR 320, dated the 16th April, 1983, (2) GSR 352, dated the 30th April, 1983, (3) GSR 314(E), dated the 27th June, 1983, (4) GSR 476(E), dated the 28th June, 1984, (3) GSR 245, dated the 23rd May, 1992, (6) GSR 83, dated the nth February, 1996. (7) GSR 101, dated the 23rd May, 1998, (8) GSR 47, dated the 13th Feb., 1999, and (9) GSR213(E)Â» dated the4 8th March, 1999. Printed by the Manager, Clow, of India IVess.
